name: Unrestricted Resource Consumption
what_it_means: The API lacks limits on requests, sizes, concurrency, or expensive operations, enabling denial of service or cost blowouts.
animated_example: All-you-can-eat buffet with no serving spoons — one person can empty everything.
quick_checks: Global/per-identity rate limits and quotas with burst control
Payload/body size and array length caps; file size/type limits
Pagination enforced; sensible max page size
Server timeouts, circuit breakers, and concurrency caps
Idempotency keys for retried operations; back-pressure at gateway
Resource-heavy endpoints require stronger auth and throttling
how_to_test: Oversized JSON bodies and deep arrays → expect 413
Deep pagination and long-running queries under load
expected_outcomes: Logs show throttling/quotas triggered with actor ID
minimal_example: POST 100MB body → 413 Payload Too Large; 1k req/min → 429 Too Many Requests
mitigation: Implement per-user and global rate limits at the API gateway layer with HTTP 429 responses and Retry-After headers — apply stricter limits to anonymous vs. authenticated callers
Enforce strict payload size caps (request body, JSON array depth, file size and type) and return 413 Payload Too Large immediately without processing the full body
Set server-side request timeouts and maximum concurrency limits; deploy circuit breakers to shed load gracefully and prevent cascading failures under traffic spikes

Apply cost-based throttling for resource-intensive operations (heavy DB queries, file processing, report generation) — require elevated quota or a separate high-cost tier rather than executing unconditionally
